data_collected: Developer portfolio details (name, geographic location, personal website link - optional)
security_practices: No volunteer access to private data
Full-time staff access restricted to technical support use cases
Daily emergency database backups deleted after a few days
Immediate deletion of personal data from servers upon user request
user_rights: Right to erase data (right to be forgotten)
Right to port data (JSON format download)
Right to object to automated decision-making
Ability to opt-out of Google Analytics
red_flags: Use of Google Ads (implies third-party tracking for monetization)
Data sharing with Google/GitHub is required if using social login
Reliance on numerous US-based third-party infrastructure providers
Collection of request metadata (though stated as temporary for debugging)
justification: The policy is exceptionally transparent and user-centric, explicitly extending GDPR protections to all global users and prohibiting the sale of data. The risk score reflects the necessary but inherent data sharing with major US-based cloud providers and the use of Google Ads/Analytics.
